item.title: James Talarico Is a Christian X-Ray
item.description: The Democratic senate candidate in Texas is teaching us about what faith really means.

item.title: The Future We Feared Is Already Here
item.description: The showdown between the Pentagon and Anthropic is a window into how unprepared we are for the questions we are facing.

item.title: Are Declining Birthrates Really a Problem?
item.description: What do declining birthrates really mean for the future? Readers respond to a front-page news article.

item.title: The Popcorn Resistance of ‘One Battle After Another’
item.description: My parents were radical. Paul Thomas Anderson’s film is anything but.

item.title: Was Tyra Banks the Villain? Or Were We?
item.description: A fashion insider and two culture writers dissect “America’s Next Top Model.”

item.title: Palestinian Citizens of Israel Are Not Safe
item.description: The Israeli government must address a crime wave in Palestinian towns.

item.title: The Badlands Hold Me as I Grieve
item.description: I imagine the birds I see are the family members I’ve lost.

item.title: The ‘Arrogance of Power’ Drives War in Iran
item.description: The Iranian people are not overthrowing their government, and the war is spreading across the region.

item.title: Going to War With Iran, Surrounded by Yes Men
item.description: Members of President Trump’s cabinet see their jobs as “total obedience to the president’s will,” the columnist Jamelle Bouie argues, and that means we should all be very worried about how the war in Iran could unfold.

item.title: One President’s Whim. A World in Crisis.
item.description: A week into Trump’s war in Iran, his strategy is still a mystery.

item.title: What Watching a Furious Hillary Clinton Showed Me
item.description: Her exasperation during her Epstein-related deposition channeled the national mood for some of us.

item.title: There Is One Crucial Reason We’re Talking About Boots on the Ground
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have no good options to address an unavoidable problem.

item.title: ‘Wuthering Heights,’ MAGA Style
item.description: Kristi and Corey cavorted in the swamp as D.H.S. got bogged down.

item.title: Trump’s Iran War and the Shadow of Iraq
item.description: The desire for a revolutionary presidency gives hubris a lot of space to work.

item.title: How Trump Views War
item.description: President Trump’s war in Iran isn’t so much a strategy as a game of “Risk,” says the Times Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie. On “The Opinions” podcast, he explains that the administration sees the world as “we big, they small,” with other nations as non-playable characters reacting to America, the “protagonists of reality.”

item.title: What Does School Choice Really Mean?
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay that argued that more school vouchers would improve public education.

item.title: One President’s Whim. A World in Crisis.
item.description: A week into Trump’s war in Iran, his strategy is still a mystery.

item.title: The Mistake That Iranians Make About America
item.description: The most likely outcome of the American-Israeli bombing campaign is not the advent of liberal democracy but something worse.

item.title: I’ve Never Been a Patriot, but Spain Standing Up to Trump Has Made Me One
item.description: There are times when it is clear what it means to stand up for what is right.

item.title: The A.I. Labor Crisis Is Coming. This Is the Solution.
item.description: America’s strength has always been its private sector. Empowering it can save us from A.I.’s worst effects on jobs.

item.title: An ‘America First' Conservative Wrestles With Trump’s War in Iran
item.description: An “America First” conservative on seeking clarity in the reasons for the military operation and restraint in foreign policy.

item.title: The Fantasy of a Comfy Retirement Has Always Been a Mirage
item.description: On the sorry state of retirement savings in the United States, and a policy that could help.

item.title: Trump’s Removal of Noem
item.description: Readers respond to the dismissal of Kristi Noem as the homeland security secretary. Also: Two ways of parking; Meta’s facial recognition.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein Was Wicked, but as the Files Show, He Was Also Banal
item.description: Just look at his philosophical opinions.

item.title: Trump’s Fantasy Is Crashing Down
item.description: The repercussions of his reckless war in Iran are just beginning.

item.title: Trump Is Playing Rope-a-Dope With Elite Law Firms
item.description: Giving in to bullies has its own costs, not least because bullies are never satisfied with just a single capitulation.

item.title: What Kristi Noem Should Do After President Trump Fired Her
item.description: It’s hard out there for a MAGA woman.

item.title: Talarico Can Win. But He Will Not Turn Texas Blue.
item.description: Texas politics is in constant churn.

item.title: Daryl Hannah: How Can ‘Love Story’ Get Away With This?
item.description: The character “Daryl Hannah” in “Love Story” is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life or my conduct — and these kinds of lies don’t go away.

item.title: After Iran: Vance vs. Carlson in 2028?
item.description: The war in Iran may have a lasting impact on the political careers of Trump’s closest allies. On “Interesting Times,” Curt Mills, the executive director of the American Conservative, shares his thoughts on how Trump’s interventionist policies might play out in the 2028 Republican primaries.

item.title: Politicians Are Trying to Control the News
item.description: The rising global attacks on the free press reveal increasingly desperate regimes attempting to snuff out attempts to find the truth.

item.title: Why the Pentagon Wants to Destroy Anthropic
item.description: The former A.I. policy adviser to the Trump White House explains why the conflict between Anthropic and the White House is so dangerous.

item.title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
item.description: How the antiwar right sees Trump’s foreign policy.

item.title: Mass Hysteria. Thousands of Jobs Lost. Just How Bad Is It Going to Get?
item.description: Michael Steinberger on how A.I.’s impact on white-collar jobs may transform politics and society.

item.title: What Are We Fighting For?
item.description: After launching a war in Iran, President Trump owes the American people a clear explanation of his objectives and possible endgame — something we have yet to receive, argues the Times Opinion writer W.J. Hennigan.

item.title: Trump ‘Can’t Say No to Israel’
item.description: Who is calling the shots in the war with Iran? Curt Mills, the executive director of the American Conservative, thinks Israeli influence is guiding Trump’s decision to go to war in the Middle East. He explains why on this episode of “Interesting Times.”

item.title: Of Course Trump Bombed Iran
item.description: The president’s unique attributes have combined with the ordinary pathologies of American foreign policy.

item.title: The Iran War, History and the Law
item.description: Readers discuss historical and legal aspects of the war. Also: Richard Nixon and a Watergate episode; America’s voting problem; scenes from “The Pitt.”

item.title: Americans Are Trying to Stop Data Centers Because They Can’t Stop A.I.
item.description: We are trying to decide what role A.I. will have in our lives.

item.title: What Is Trump Up To in Iran?
item.description: Well, that was a week.

item.title: It’s the Troll vs. the Bore in the G.O.P. Texas Senate Runoff
item.description: The state needs intelligent and responsible political leadership, but will it get it?

item.title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
item.description: How the antiwar right sees Trump’s foreign policy.

item.title: Trump Is Playing a Dangerous Game With Iran
item.description: Hungary. Cuba. Afghanistan. Iraq. Now Iran?

item.title: Alabama Is About to Commit a Shocking Death Row Injustice
item.description: Governor Ivey, Sonny Burton is not a killer. In his case, justice must include mercy.

item.title: What Our Industrial Food System Can’t Do
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay, “Stop Worrying, and Learn to Love Industrial Food.”

item.title: I Worked for Block. Its A.I. Job Cuts Aren’t What They Seem.
item.description: Is Block’s announcement just a convenient and flashy new cover for typical corporate downsizing?
